Description: S3 static website hosting and CORS configuration examples

Example1_StaticWebsiteHosting.Description: Host a static website (React, Angular, Vue) on S3 behind CloudFront

BucketPolicy.Description: CloudFront Origin Access Control policy — only CloudFront can access the bucket

Example2_CORS_APIAccess.Description: Allow a frontend app (https://myapp.example.com) to make API requests to S3 (e.g., direct uploads via presigned URLs)

Example3_CORS_MultipleOrigins.Description: Allow multiple origins (dev, staging, prod) to access S3

Example4_PresignedURL_Upload.Description: Generate a presigned URL for direct client-to-S3 upload — no server proxy needed
Example4_PresignedURL_Upload.Python_Boto3:
import boto3

s3 = boto3.client('s3')

url = s3.generate_presigned_url(
    'put_object',
    Params={
        'Bucket': 'my-upload-bucket',
        'Key': 'uploads/user123/photo.jpg',
        'ContentType': 'image/jpeg'
    },
    ExpiresIn=3600  # 1 hour
)

print(url)  # Give this URL to the frontend

Example5_PresignedURL_Download.Description: Generate a presigned URL for temporary download access to a private object
Example5_PresignedURL_Download.Python_Boto3:
import boto3

s3 = boto3.client('s3')

url = s3.generate_presigned_url(
    'get_object',
    Params={
        'Bucket': 'my-private-bucket',
        'Key': 'reports/quarterly-2024.pdf'
    },
    ExpiresIn=900  # 15 minutes
)

print(url)
Example5_PresignedURL_Download.Note: Presigned URLs inherit the permissions of the IAM user/role that created them. They expire after the specified time.

StaticWebsite_Architecture.Flow: Route 53 (custom domain) → CloudFront (CDN + HTTPS + WAF) → S3 (origin, private, OAC) → API Gateway (for dynamic APIs)
Steps: 1. Create S3 bucket (Block Public Access ON — CloudFront handles access)
2. Upload built frontend (npm run build → upload dist/ to S3)
3. Create CloudFront distribution with S3 origin + Origin Access Control
4. Add custom domain in CloudFront + ACM certificate (us-east-1)
5. Create Route 53 alias record pointing to CloudFront
6. Add WAF Web ACL to CloudFront for security
7. Set up CloudFront cache invalidation in CI/CD (after deploy)

AWS_CLI_Commands.EnableWebsite: aws s3 website s3://my-bucket/ --index-document index.html --error-document error.html

InterviewTip: The S3 + CloudFront static website pattern is asked in almost every interview. Key points: (1) NEVER enable S3 website hosting endpoint for production — use CloudFront + OAC instead (HTTPS, caching, WAF). (2) S3 website endpoint is HTTP only. (3) Use presigned URLs for private file access, not public buckets. (4) CORS is needed when your frontend domain differs from S3/API domain.
